groups.context_description: Term names divine indignation as sustained moral disposition — the ongoing inner displeasure of God toward wickedness, felt daily
Term names human indignation as responsive inner emotion — the emotional-moral reaction of moral displeasure arising from provocation or perceived injustice
Term names what God finds morally detestable in human inner character and valuation — the hidden life assessed by divine moral perception
Term names the abominable inner character produced by the gap between professed knowledge of God and actual conduct
Term names the inner disposition of revulsion toward evil — what a person claims as morally repugnant, whether genuinely or hypocritically held
Term names the person whose inner moral character is itself the object of divine revulsion — character as abhorrent in God's sight
Term names the fearsome quality that breeds inner self-deception — pride of heart that trusts in its own terrible reputation rather than God
Term names the ritual category of defilement as a boundary of inner spiritual integrity — what the inwardly devoted person refuses as incompatible with devotion to God
Term names the inner orientation of heart, soul, and eyes toward detestable objects — gaze, desire, and delight directed at idolatrous things
Term names the inner character conformation that results from attachment to detestable things — the person becoming like what they love
Term names the commanded inner revulsion — the active rejection of what is unclean as an expression of obedience and moral self-preservation
Term names the reflexive self-defilement — the inner corruption that results from transgression, making the person themselves detestable
Term names divine non-revulsion toward the afflicted — God does not abhor those who suffer; affliction does not make a person detestable to God
Term names the objects of idolatrous inner devotion — the detestable things toward which misdirected worship is oriented
Term names divine inner loathing directed specifically at pride — God abhors the arrogance of Jacob
Term names divine moral assessment of inner character as detestable — the wicked person's thoughts, heart, and dispositions as abomination before God
Term names worship as abominable when it arises from a corrupted inner moral condition — the sacrifices of the wicked are detestable
Term names the failure of inner shame — the conscience that can no longer feel appropriate disgrace over its own abomination
Term names inner grief and self-loathing as response to abomination — the inner movement of sorrow over one's own detestable conduct as the beginning of restoration
Term names the categories of conduct that God finds abominable — violations of holiness that offend divine character and defile the person
Term names the deep inner disposition of abhorrence toward falsehood and evil — the morally alert person's visceral revulsion paired with love of what is true
Term names the inverted disposition of abhorrence — the capacity for moral revulsion directed against justice and truth rather than evil
Term names divine sustained inner abhorrence toward those who have persistently corrupted themselves or violated covenant
Term names the covenantal act whose content is the writing of divine law on the inner mind and heart — inner transformation as the substance of the new covenant
Term names purposive inner intention — the act of setting a planned goal before the will, whether human or divine
Term names the odium-quality of inner moral failure — the stench-metaphor expressing how wickedness and folly produce corruption visible to others
Salvation of the inner person — saved by grace through faith
Healing through faith — the inner instrument of salvation in physical need
Cry for rescue — inner-being posture of dependent appeal
The Savior as the object of inner-being trust, joy, and hope
The Savior as Jesus — his identity and the inner-being transformation he brings
Salvation as God's inner-being transformative gift seen and received
Reckless inner orientation of self-squandering
Debauchery as the inner orientation of dissolute self-indulgence, contrasted with Spirit-filling
Defilement as the corrupting of inner conscience and moral purity
Defilement of spirit — inner-being corruption requiring cleansing and holiness
Term names wandering or fleeing as an inner experience — restlessness of sleep fleeing, the anguish of spiritual straying, or the inner alienation expressed through wandering
Term names the tossing of the body in sleeplessness — an outward expression of deep inner restlessness and suffering
Term names the filth that sin produces — the moral defilement following grievous sin, bringing shame and contempt, met by inner groaning and the turning away of the face
Term names the unclean spirit as a spiritual entity that inhabits the inner space of the person — its presence produces inner captivity; its expulsion by Christ restores the person's inner freedom
Term names uncleanness as a moral or ritual condition of the person — the inner state of being separated from holiness and divine fellowship
Term names the physical or ritual act of cleansing — the healing of leprosy or the purification of ritual impurity, which serves as the context of faith and divine action
Term names the inner cleansing — the purification of the conscience, heart, hands, and soul from sin and defilement
Term names purity as an inner quality of the person — a clean heart, clear conscience, or pure motivation, which is the condition of knowing and serving God
Term names cleanness as a status conferred by Christ or declared by God — the removal of defilement so that the person stands as clean before God
Term names the act of purification — whether ritual or atoning, naming the process by which impurity is removed from the person before God
Term names ritual defilement of a person — becoming unclean through contact with death, bodily discharge, or unclean things, requiring cleansing before re-entering community and worship
Term names moral self-defilement — the active corruption of oneself through idolatry, sexual sin, or unfaithfulness, which defiles the inner person, sacred space, and the land
Term names uncleanness as a condition separating the person from God and community — whether ritual impurity requiring cleansing or moral/spiritual defilement requiring divine removal
Term names menstrual impurity as a ritual condition — the state that separates the person from normal community life and requires purification
Term names ritual or moral impurity extended beyond the menstrual context — the defilement of a land, a city, or a person through sin and idolatry, requiring cleansing
Term names impurity as an inner moral condition — the state of the heart, desires, and will oriented away from holiness toward moral corruption
Term names ritual uncleanness as a condition of the person — the state of being separated from worship and community, calling for recognition and cleansing
Term names moral uncleanness as an inner condition — the comprehensive defilement of the person before God through sin, idolatry, or universal human unworthiness
Term names crushing as an inner moral reality — the oppression that degrades and destroys the vulnerable, and the gentleness that refuses to crush the bruised
Term names oppression as the outward expression of an inward orientation — the eyes and heart fixed wholly on dishonest gain and violence
Term names Israel's spiritual fornication — the inner disposition of disordered desire driving pursuit of foreign gods and alliances, defiling the covenantal relationship with God
Term names the craving or lust for tribute as an inner disposition of greed that God opposes
Term names animal-like instinctual lust that cannot be restrained — used as a metaphor for Israel's insatiable craving for foreign gods
Term names bronze as a metaphor for hardened moral character, inescapable bondage, or the inner resolve of divine strengthening — the material pointing to an inner state
Term names blasphemy against God — the verbal expression of an inner orientation of contempt, defiance, or rejection directed at God, constituting the most serious inner-spiritual transgression
Term names slander or verbal contempt directed at persons — an act of inner contempt expressed as damaging speech, or the experience of being maliciously defamed
Term names slander as an act of malicious speech arising from inner hostility — a moral failure that damages relational integrity
Term names the act of slandering — going about with malicious speech, an inner act of verbal contempt that violates relational integrity
Term names slander as an act of inner-relational betrayal — speaking against one's own kin, the sharpest form of contemptuous speech within intimate community
Term names the act of bringing a false or malicious report — the verbal expression of an inner orientation of contempt, fear, or faithlessness that damages others and bears inner-moral consequence
Term names the act of slandering — verbal contempt directed at another that incurs moral guilt before God and community
Term names the act of going about as a slanderer — the inner-moral condition of one whose habitual speech expresses contempt and deception toward others
Term names slander as an experience endured — the condition of being maliciously defamed, received as a test of inner faithfulness
Term names reviling as an act of verbal abuse — the inner-relational act of attacking another with contemptuous speech, and the tested inner response of non-retaliation
Term names the character or act of being blasphemous — the inner orientation of contempt toward God expressed in speech or action
Term names the slanderer as a character — one whose inner orientation is expressed through malicious speech directed at others
Term is used as a metaphor for the course and direction of a person's moral-spiritual life — feet as the expression of inner orientation, choice, and moral path
Term is used in relational acts of submission, supplication, or devotion — falling at someone's feet as an expression of the inner orientation of humility, desperation, or gratitude
Term is used metaphorically for the inner capacity of perseverance under testing — the challenge of competing on foot as a figure for the inner demands of prophetic calling
Term names the intimate act of teaching to walk — evoking the inner-relational dynamic between the caring teacher-parent and the dependent learner, with the poignancy of unrecognised nurture
Temptation as a condition of inner exposure — the state of being vulnerable to the solicitation of desire, evil, or the devil; the subject of prayer, warning, and apostolic concern
Testing/trials as the inner-being context of faith formation — the experience of trial that pressures faith, endurance, and hope, producing either steadfastness or falling away
Testing as expression of inner disposition — the act of testing Jesus or God expressing hostility, unbelief, or the hardening of the heart; including self-examination and the testing of faith

Temptation as assault on the inner being — the devil's solicitation of desire, weakness, and the will, including Jesus's temptation in the wilderness and the believer's exposure to temptation through lack of self-control
Testing God — the inner disposition of demanding proof from God, testing the limit of divine faithfulness; including the prohibition of this inner orientation
Attempting as expression of inner desire or hostility — acts of trying that originate in changed inner orientation or inner hostility
Moral-cognitive inexperience — the state of being unskilled in the word of righteousness, designating inner formation deficit
Spiritual/moral stumbling — the inner act of falling in faith, disobedience, or moral failing; including stumbling because the light is not within
The stumbling block as moral-faith obstacle — something that causes another to stumble in faith, conscience, or moral standing; including the stone of stumbling as a theological category
Moral stumbling and spiritual failing — the inner act of falling in the law, in speech, or in steadfastness of calling
Moral blamelessness and clear conscience — the inner state of moral integrity described as the absence of stumbling before God and others
Divine preservation from spiritual falling — the blameless inner condition in which one stands before God's glory
Whoredom as a spiritual condition — the spirit of whoredom within, leading Israel astray
Spiritual whoredom — Israel/Judah "whoring after" foreign gods, forsaking the Lord; covenantal heart-departure
"A spirit of whoredom" — whoredom as a driving inner disposition and inner-being faculty
"Your heart and your own eyes, which you are inclined to whore after" — the inner mechanism of desire as the root of whoredom
Literal fornication as inner-being act — the will and desire engaged in sexual unfaithfulness
Ezekiel's "whoring heart" allegory — inner-being characterisation of Jerusalem/Israel's spiritual condition
Whoredom as inner-being corruption — defilement of the relational space with God; the act that takes away understanding
Term names malice as an inner disposition of the soul — the soul's rejoicing in another's destruction, driven by deep-seated enmity and contempt
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored
